title: angelica atropurpurea
intro: Angelica atropurpurea, also known as purple angelica, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its tall, purple-stemmed flower spikes and large, umbrella-like leaves. The roots of the plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral compounds. The health benefits associated with Angelica atropurpurea include reducing fever and inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Angelica atropurpurea is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or tea, and is also used in herbal infusions and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
